methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $200M total raised proxy.

private.name: Bedrock Ocean
private.rationale: Same sector (Ocean & Maritime), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Ulysses Ecosystem Engineering
private.rationale: Same sector (Ocean & Maritime), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Havoc AI is a Series A-stage frontier-tech company operating in the Ocean & Maritime sector. The company has raised $200M to date. The available comp universe comprises two private peers—Bedrock Ocean and Ulysses Ecosystem Engineering—with no direct public comparables identified in the sector.

Given the limited comp coverage, valuation relies on stage-progression methodology. This approach applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage advancement, anchored to the $200M total raised as a proxy for current value. This method yields an implied valuation of $400M. The stage-progression framework is applicable here given Havoc AI's defined funding history and clear placement within the venture capital progression pathway.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $340M (low) to $460M (high), with a median of $400M. The $340M–$460M band reflects reasonable variance around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties center on the absence of public-market benchmarks and the limited private peer set, which constrains cross-validation. Revenue run-rate, customer concentration, and technology differentiation relative to Bedrock Ocean and Ulysses Ecosystem Engineering remain material valuation drivers not addressed in available data. Refinement of this range would benefit from expanded comp analysis and unit economics disclosure.
